content.problemStatement:
A school is conducting an exam, and it is essential for students to achieve a minimum score to pass. 

The goal is to determine if a student has successfully passed the exam based on their score.

**Requirements:**


Define the minimum passing mark as **40**.
Take the student's score as input from the user.
Use an if statement to check if the student's score is greater than or equal to the passing mark.
If the score meets or exceeds the passing mark, print: **"You passed the exam!"**. If the score is below the passing mark, the program should do nothing.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 1000), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer score (0 ≤ score ≤ 100).
content.outputFormat: For each test case with score ≥ 40, output the line: You passed the exam! (without quotes). Do not output anything for scores below 40.

sampleTestCases.explanation: First score 45 ≥ 40, so the message is printed. Second score 35 < 40, so nothing is printed.
Score equals the passing mark, therefore the message is printed.

sampleTestCases.output:
You passed the exam!
You passed the exam!
sampleTestCases.explanation: 39 is below the threshold – no output. 40 and 41 meet or exceed the threshold, so the message appears twice.

hidden.label: maximum boundary
hidden.expectedOutput:
You passed the exam!
You passed the exam!
You passed the exam!

hidden.label: general case 1
hidden.expectedOutput:
You passed the exam!
You passed the exam!
You passed the exam!

solution.approach: Read the integer T. Loop T times reading each score. Compare the score with the constant passing mark 40. If the score is greater than or equal to 40, print the required message. The algorithm is O(T) time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – each test case is processed in constant time.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few scalar variables are used besides the output buffer.
